title: auto-update: log errors checking for updates
body:
When checking for new images on registries, do not suppress            
and debug-log errors but return them.  Other images and units          
will still be processed.                                               
                                                                       
That is important to a) stop hiding flaky registries (see #18321)      
                     b) mark the updates as `failed` instead of `false`

Closes: #18321

Finally, be sure to sign commits with your real name. Since by opening
a PR you already have commits, you can add signatures if needed with
something like `git commit -s --amend`.

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *vrothberg* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [vrothberg] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

> I am not a huge fan of the errors logs but then still exiting with 0. While I agree that we should continue and try to do as much as possible it would still make sense to exit > 0. At the very least to make sure the systemd auto-update unit will reflect the error state accordingly.

I'll take another look after lunch.  I was torn in between keeping it as is and returning an error and declaring the update `failed` instead of `false`.
LGTM. It's an unfortunate tradeoff, but I agree that it's better to let the caller deal with (and see) errors.
